parent_entity.value: Restaurant Brands International Limited Partnership (RBILP), controlled by Restaurant Brands International Inc. (RBI)

franchising_since.value: September 2022 (as BKC LLC); 1954 as predecessor BK Corporation

formats.notes: All ranges exclude real estate acquisition costs. Traditional includes optional 2-story playground ($0-$245,000).

entry_paths.name: Development Agreement
entry_paths.exclusivity: None (geographic Territory granted but not exclusive)

entry_paths.name: Crown Your Career Program
entry_paths.deposit: Purchase price (may be financed by BKC)

refundability.value: All fees nonrefundable except: TRA/MTRA deposit refundable if BKC proposes Target Area development and franchisee declines; Carrols deposit refundable within 30 days

advertising_contribution.value: Up to 4.5% of monthly Gross Sales (set at 4.5% as of 1/1/2025)

investment_spending_local_marketing.value: Up to 2.0% of Gross Sales (determined by DMA franchisees)

late_charges.value: 18% per annum or max legal rate

combined_mandatory_minimum.value: 9.0% of Gross Sales (royalty 4.5% + advertising 4.5%) before rent, digital, local marketing, and other assessments
combined_mandatory_minimum.notes: Derived from Item 6 table. Actual combined burden is higher when including digital services fee, investment spending, BK University, service desk, and foundation contributions.

royalty_reduction_programs.value: Multiple programs reduce royalty: 2024 DIP (2.5%→4.5% over 6 years), Development Agreement (A/B grade: 2.5% reduction Year 1, declining), RTF2 (4.5%-6.0% elected). D/F operators get no reductions.

remodel_penalty_surcharge.value: +3.0% royalty surcharge on all Gross Sales for missed remodel deadlines under RTF2, RTF1, or Midterm Remodel Forbearance

required_before_opening.value: Yes — franchisee or appropriate individual(s) must complete BKC training program before opening

training_location.value: Miami, FL or other BKC-designated locations; In-Restaurant Training at authorized Training Restaurants

servsafe_required.value: Yes — Operating Partner/Managing Owner, restaurant manager, and Managing Director must complete ServSafe before opening

training_table_with_subjects_and_hours.notes: No formal training program table with Subject/Hours/Classroom columns was found in Item 11. Training described narratively only.

pos_and_kiosks.value: Franchisee must purchase own POS, inventory control, and freestanding kiosks. Estimated $20,000-$25,000.

digital_menu_boards.value: Required. Sole-source supplier. If no outdoor digital menu board, must pay $200-$300/month for static menu board kit.

digital_app_services.value: Mandatory Digital App Services Agreement (Exhibit V). BKC is sole supplier of ordering/delivery/loyalty platforms. Fee: $110/mo + 1% digital sales, capped $4,500/yr.
digital_app_services.notes: Agreement existence and fee confirmed. Full operative terms in Exhibit V not directly surfaced.

network_services.value: Sole-source supplier. Required for all restaurants.

loyalty_program.value: Mandatory participation in Royal Perks Loyalty Program via BKC technology platforms. Customers earn/redeem Crowns at all locations.

delivery_platforms.value: Must offer delivery via BKC-approved third-party aggregators (e.g., Uber Eats) in serviced areas. All providers must be BKC-approved.

bkc_revenue_from_franchisee_purchases.value: Approximately $227 million in 2024 (~16% of BKC total revenues of ~$1.427 billion)
bkc_revenue_from_franchisee_purchases.notes: From unaudited internal BKC statements, not RBI consolidated.

supplier_rebates_to_ad_fund.value: ~8.4% of consolidated advertising fund revenue in 2024

purchasing_cooperative.value: RSI (Restaurant Services Inc.) — not-for-profit independent cooperative. 21-member Board (18 franchisees, 1 BKC, 1 minority, 1 independent). Issues patronage dividends.

officer_stock_holdings_in_suppliers.value: BKC officers own publicly traded shares in General Mills, Johnson & Johnson, Kraft Heinz, and PepsiCo (all approved suppliers)

exclusive_territory_granted.value: No. Explicitly stated: 'You will not receive an exclusive territory.'

franchise_agreement_territory.value: Single location only. No area, territory, or protected customer base.

development_agreement_territory.value: Geographic Territory granted but not exclusive. BKC may still develop or franchise other restaurants in the Territory.

alternative_distribution_channels.value: BKC reserves all rights to sell BK branded products via grocery, convenience, internet, direct marketing, or any other channel in any territory. No compensation to franchisee.

affiliate_brand_competition.value: Tim Hortons, Popeyes, and Firehouse Subs may operate in same markets. BKC not responsible for resolving inter-brand conflicts.

relocation_right.value: No right to relocate without BKC's prior written approval

during_term.value: May not own, operate, or have any interest in any other hamburger business. May not use BK System, marks, or trade secrets outside BK operations.

during_term_cyc_enhancement.value: Crown Your Career: no other business activity until later of 5th anniversary or loan repayment

post_term.value: 1 year after termination/expiration, within 2 miles of the restaurant — no ownership, operation, or interest in any hamburger business

corporate_addendum_exception.value: Certain Contract Feeder franchisees under Corporate Addendum may operate competing fast-food businesses under limited conditions

term.value: 20 years for freestanding traditional. May be shorter for non-traditional or where property control is shorter.

renewal.value: No right of renewal. Option for successor FA (up to 20 years) conditioned on: compliance, property control, current requirements met, BKC approval, general release, signing then-current FA (terms may materially differ), successor fee, building upgrades, inspections. No successor for Big-Box or Delivery Restaurants.

transfer_conditions.value: BKC consent required. Buyer approved, training complete, all monies current, transfer fee ($2,000 + $500/additional), new franchisee training fee ($7,500), assignment of existing FA or signing then-current FA, general release by seller, inspection, current image compliance.

right_of_first_refusal.value: BKC and designee have right to purchase restaurant or any interest on same terms as third-party offer

termination_by_franchisee.value: No contractual right; only as permitted by applicable state law

value.5_days: Operational, quality, health/sanitation standards; sale of unapproved products; abandonment; insurance
value.10_days: Non-payment of royalty or advertising fees
value.30_days: Late reports; unapproved equipment/uniforms/decor; maintenance failures; failure to remodel; deleterious behavior; unsatisfied judgment; failure to restore; non-compliance with OP/MO/MD requirements
value.60_days: Failure to pay bills; failure to comply with any other FA, lease, or related agreement provision
value.180_days: Failure to meet minimum financial criteria per BKC financial policy

non_curable_defaults.value: Bankruptcy; conviction of indictable offense (>1yr) or fraud; unfair competition; false statements; understatement of Gross Sales; failure to close for health/safety; unauthorized transfer; repeated breaches after notice; delivery aggregator termination (Delivery Restaurants); RTF program default

cross_defaults.value: CYC: default under any BK FA = default under all CYC agreements. Carrols Refranchise: default under any lease or agreement for any BK Restaurant in the refranchising transaction = default under this FA.

post_termination_obligations.value: Cease trademark use; de-identify restaurant; pay all amounts due including royalties and advertising for remainder of term; return MOD Manual and proprietary information

forum.value: US District Court for Southern District of Florida, or state court in Miami-Dade County, FL (subject to state law)

death_or_disability.value: Heir must be approved or sell within 12 months. BKC option to operate/manage in interim.

liquidated_damages.notes: Item 17 summary does not reference liquidated damages. Post-termination royalty/advertising obligation for remainder of term may function similarly. Full FA text (Exhibits D1/D2) not reviewed.

item_18_public_figures.value: BKC does not use any public figures to promote or recommend its franchise.

section_b_remodel_uplift.sample_definition: Traditional Restaurants, CAPEX $650,000+, remodeled to Modern Image between 1/1/2018 and 12/31/2023

multi_year_persistence.note: Uplift persists and slightly increases over 3 years

cost_data_disclosed.value: No. Sales figures do not reflect costs of sales, operating expenses, or other costs. No income, profit, or cost data disclosed.

item_20_outlet_summary.carrols_acquisition_note: On May 16, 2024, RBI acquired Carrols Restaurant Group (1,023 restaurants). This single transaction accounts for nearly all 2024 reacquisitions and makes year-over-year comparisons misleading without context.
item_20_outlet_summary.franchisee_gag_clause: Some current and former franchisees signed provisions restricting their ability to speak openly about their experience in the last three fiscal years.

audit_opinion.value: Clean opinion on both financial statements and internal controls for RBI and RBILP

guarantor_structure.value: RBILP guarantees BKC obligations for franchisees in CA, IL, MD, ND, RI, VA, WA. RBI guarantees for all other states.

financial_entity.value: Restaurant Brands International Inc. (RBI) and Restaurant Brands International Limited Partnership (RBILP) — consolidated across all four brands. No standalone BKC financials.

key_exhibits.title: Reclaim the Flame 2 Master Program Agreement
Crown Your Career Program (FA addenda, note, security)

unresolveds.description: No training program table found in Item 11. Training described narratively. Possible that a formal table exists in Exhibits D1/D2 or Exhibit U (MOD Manual TOC).
unresolveds.impact: Cannot independently verify training hours, subjects, or classroom/on-the-job split.

unresolveds.description: Item 19 references Modern Image vs Legacy Image comparison within Section A sales distributions (page 99), but no separate table for this comparison was located. The comparison may be embedded in the narrative rather than a standalone table.
unresolveds.impact: Cannot confirm whether Modern Image restaurants outperform Legacy Image in current AUV.

unresolveds.description: Guarantee of Performance documents (RBI and RBILP) are referenced in Item 21 as 'included at Exhibit Q' but no separately-headed guarantee pages were found in the Exhibit Q scan. They may be interleaved with or follow the financial statement notes.
unresolveds.impact: Guarantee text and scope not independently verified beyond Item 21 description.

unresolveds.description: Items 2, 3, and 4 (Business Experience, Litigation, Bankruptcy) were not read in detail. Item 3 spans 8 pages (20-27) and appears to contain substantial litigation history.
unresolveds.impact: Cannot assess litigation risk, key management backgrounds, or bankruptcy history.

unresolveds.description: BKC standalone revenue figure ($1.427 billion) appears in Item 8 from unaudited internal financial statements, but no standalone BKC financial statements are included in the FDD. The relationship between this figure and the consolidated RBI figures is not reconciled.
unresolveds.impact: Cannot isolate BKC-specific financial performance from the RBI consolidated statements.

unresolveds.description: Full operative text of Franchise Agreements (D1: 48 pages, D2: 42 pages), Lease/Sublease (G1: 41 pages), and Development Agreement (M1: 24 pages) was not read. Item 17 summaries were used instead.
unresolveds.impact: Specific contract provisions, defined terms, and detailed mechanics may differ from Item 17 summaries.

unresolveds.description: Fuel the Flame 4-Wall EBITDA benchmark ($230,000) referenced in Item 6 is 'as calculated by BKC in accordance with the terms of such agreement.' The actual calculation methodology is in Exhibit Y1 which was not read.
unresolveds.impact: Cannot verify how 4-Wall EBITDA is defined or what costs are included/excluded.

unresolveds.description: Value Menu pricing restriction: BKC can set maximum prices on Value Menu items. Specific current Value Menu items and maximum prices are not disclosed in the FDD Items reviewed.
unresolveds.impact: Cannot assess margin impact of Value Menu pricing caps.

contradictions.description: Item 7 cover page states total investment range as '$348,400 to $4,705,600' but this spans all facility types (Big-Box low to Traditional Freestanding high). The cover page does not clarify this is a multi-format range, which could mislead a reader into thinking a single format has this range.
contradictions.field_b: Item 7 tables show 6 distinct formats with non-overlapping ranges
contradictions.resolution: Not a true contradiction — the cover page range is the combined min/max across all formats. But the presentation without format context is potentially misleading.

contradictions.description: Item 20 Table 3 total for 2024 shows 'Reacquired by Franchisor: 1,061' but Item 20 Table 4 also shows 'Reacquired from Franchisee: 1,061.' The Carrols acquisition asterisk note says '1,023 franchised Restaurants' became company-owned. The difference between 1,061 and 1,023 (38 units) is unexplained — it may represent additional non-Carrols reacquisitions or timing differences.
contradictions.resolution: Not resolved. The 38-unit gap may represent pre-Carrols or non-Carrols reacquisitions in 2024 (e.g., Utah 17 in 2023, Montana 12 in 2023 were pre-Carrols). Some of the 2024 reacquisitions outside Carrols states are likely separate transactions.

contradictions.description: Item 6 Footnote 2 states 'Base Fee means the greater of 4.5% of Gross Sales or the then-current standard (undiscounted) royalty fee required for new franchisees.' This implies the standard royalty could exceed 4.5% in the future, even though 4.5% is presented as the standard rate throughout the FDD.
contradictions.field_b: Base Fee definition allows for future increase above 4.5%
contradictions.resolution: Not a contradiction but a forward-looking risk. The Base Fee definition creates a mechanism for the effective royalty floor to increase without amending the Franchise Agreement.
